title: helianthemum × coronadoi nothosubsp. coronadoi
intro: Helianthemum × coronadoi nothosubsp. coronadoi, also known as a hybrid of rockrose or dune sunrose, is a plant species that belongs to the family Cistaceae and is native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Helianthemum × coronadoi nothosubsp. coronadoi include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and topical creams to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
